paper_title: Investigating the consistency between proxies and between proxies and models using data assimilation: a mid-Holocene case study

methods.description: This method involves using a particle filter with resampling to assimilate data into climate simulations. It is used to improve the accuracy of climate model predictions by incorporating paleodata and maintaining a constant number of simulation particles.

steps.description: An ensemble of 96 simulations, also referred to as particles or 20 ensemble members, is first initiated from slightly different states obtained by perturbing the surface temperature of an equilibrium experiment under the same conditions as the simulation without data assimilation.
steps.searchable_summary: initialize ensemble of simulations with perturbed surface temperatures

steps.description: After the assimilation step, which is six months here, the likelihood of each member of the difference between mid-Holocene and modern conditions is computed for all the locations and months for which paleodata is available.
steps.searchable_summary: compute likelihood of simulation members based on paleodata
outputs: likelihood values for each simulation member

steps.description: The particles that have the largest likelihood are retained. The other ensemble members are rejected.
The remaining particles are resampled a number of times proportional to their likelihood in order to keep the number of particles constant and avoid a degenerative issue.

A small perturbation of surface temperature is then added to the initial conditions of the members that have been sampled more than once, and the particles are propagated forward in time using the climate model.
steps.searchable_summary: add perturbation and propagate particles forward in time

steps.description: The whole procedure is repeated until the end of the simulated period (which corresponds to 400 yr here).
steps.searchable_summary: repeat procedure for entire simulation period

methods.description: This method describes the design of mid-Holocene simulations with data assimilation to compare different proxy data and model results. It aims to identify the signal brought by different subsets of data and assess the compatibility between model physics and proxies.

steps.description: Three mid-Holocene simulations with data assimilation are performed and compared to a mid-Holocene reference simulation realized with LOVECLIM, named NODATA.
steps.searchable_summary: perform and compare mid-Holocene simulations with and without data assimilation

steps.description: In two simulations either the continental or the oceanic proxies are assimilated and in one simulation these proxies are assimilated together.
steps.searchable_summary: assimilate continental and oceanic proxies in simulations

steps.description: A simulation spanning the reference period (950 to 450 yr BP) is required as the likelihood in the data assimilation process compares proxy anomalies with modeled anomalies.
steps.searchable_summary: perform reference period simulation for likelihood comparison

steps.description: Simulations performed with GCMs to allow the comparison of the LOVECLIM results with the ones from three state-of-the-art models: BCC-CSM1-1, CSIRO-Mk3L-1-2 and MPI-ESM-P.
steps.searchable_summary: compare LOVECLIM results with GCM simulations
